hard_rules.rule: Synthetic or fallback data may never satisfy a production or live decision gate.
UNAVAILABLE, ERROR, STALE, NOT_CONFIGURED, or NO_DATA must not generate a current market claim or active order instruction.
Observed, inferred, disputed, unknown, and model-dependent evidence must remain visibly separate.
Options, TRF prints, broker flow, transfers, short volume, or price/volume alone must not be labeled as beneficial-owner intent.
Conviction or qualitative confidence must not be used as calibrated probability.
Expected value requires calibrated probabilities plus costs, slippage, capacity, and horizon.
No single formula or bottleneck definition may be assumed valid across all markets.
IHSG may not emit a ticker-level short execution instruction.
No active instruction without trigger, entry state, trade invalidation, thesis invalidation, liquidity, costs, and capacity.
Predictive evidence requires point-in-time availability, revision vintage, survivor-free universe treatment, and correct event timestamps.
Predictive promotion requires market-specific strong baseline, purged/embargoed walk-forward evidence, costs, multiple-testing control, ablation, and failure review.
A hidden lockbox may be opened once only after formulas, parameters, universe, and evaluation policy are frozen.
No production claim without matured prospective outcomes under a frozen policy.
Compile, UI, parser, startup, or regression PASS does not prove predictive edge or profitability.
Even complete evidence gates grant limited-production eligibility only; they never authorize autonomous orders.
Decision quality must be recorded before outcome and judged separately from P&L.
Contradictory evidence, rejected alternatives, and uncertainty must be logged rather than hidden.
Every market adapter must include winner and loser autopsy, why the mechanism worked, and which critical conditions persist.
Every edge claim must explain the likely other side, its constraints and horizon, and why the trade is favorable without assuming stupidity.

Options/Greeks are enabled only for a supported product, instrument and current option-specific feed. IHSG options are disabled; crypto is per underlying/venue; commodity options are options-on-futures; FX requires listed options or an OTC vol surface.
Public option open interest or call/put type must not be assigned a dealer sign. Signed GEX, vanna, charm and hedge direction require explicit auditable inventory ownership.
Generic OHLCV context may rank research attention but may not emit ticker-level directional watches or capital instructions.
Technical reference geometry must be labeled separately from scenario valuation, remaining return and fair value.
Alpha potential remains withheld until direct value capture, point-in-time economic assumptions, dilution/supply, scenario ranges and calibrated probabilities are available.
Adaptive HMM/Wasserstein models are challengers against simple baselines under identical PIT data, costs, constraints and repeated walk-forward splits; reported paper Sharpe is not production proof.
A recent date or changed official page is not source verification. Current-development claims require explicit human review and never create automatic direction.
Order-flow imbalance is venue-specific execution research. Contemporaneous price impact must not be relabeled as durable next-move prediction or institutional intent.
